---
title: 'Voiceover Research'
date: '2020-03-18T23:46:00.0010000+00:00'
draft: true
blogger_blog: 'Towdah: tō·dä'''
blogger_status: 'LIVE'
blogger_post_id: '2282877529024510516'
blogger_filename: '/2020/03/voiceover-research.html'
blogger_source: 'takeout-20260223T235336Z-3-001.zip:Takeout/Blogger/Blogs/Towdah_ tō·dä_/feed.atom'
categories:
  - 'Voiceover'
---

<br />
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><span style="font-size: 14.0pt;"><a href="https://manual.audacityteam.org/index.html">Audacity 2.3.3 Manual</a><o:p></o:p></span></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><u><span style="font-size: 14.0pt;">Voiceover Research<o:p></o:p></span></u></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://www.voices.com/blog/soundproof-your-space/">Voice
Over Home Studio – How to Soundproof a Room</a><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
YouTube: <a href="https://youtu.be/JPYt10zrclQ">How Sound
Works (In Rooms)</a><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b>1. Mattress Covers.</b> Egg crate mattress covers are an
economical way to obtain soundproofing and work similarly to acoustic foam.
They can be found at many discount supply stores and often in thrift stores.
They can easily be installed by gluing or stapling them to your walls.<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b>2. Carpeting. </b>The thicker the better! It’s not just
for flooring either. You can attach carpet to your walls or cut strips of
carpeting and attach them to the seams around windows and doors to dampen the
noise coming in from outside. Go to your local flooring company and ask about
purchasing their miscuts.<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b>3. Sound Baffles. </b>These are barriers that stop the
reverberation in a room. Attach sheets or pieces of foam at various points
across your ceiling to reduce airborne sound. They don’t need to touch the
floor to make significant impact and are extra items you likely have around
your home.<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
YouTube: <a href="https://youtu.be/N_bAzj7zdDk">How to Mix a
Voice Over with Music without Ducking</a><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://www.voices.com/blog/presets_and_templates_for_audio_recording_voice-overs/">4
Recommended settings for DAW Templates:</a> <o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l1 level1 lfo1; tab-stops: list .5in;"><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Have an empty track
     ready to go with preset plugins already set up<o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l1 level1 lfo1; tab-stops: list .5in;"><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Presets may include some
     light EQ to bring out the best qualities in your voice<o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l1 level1 lfo1; tab-stops: list .5in;"><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Light compression to
     ensure your voice isn’t varying too much in volume<o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l1 level1 lfo1; tab-stops: list .5in;"><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Perhaps even a little
     de-ess if the sibilance in your voice comes off a little intense after
     some light compression<o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://www.voices.com/blog/budgeting-home-recording-studio-equipment/">5.
Home Recording Studio Gear Tip: Buy Used!</a><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
There is no shortage of second-hand equipment looking for a
good home. Cut down some of your costs by purchasing gently used recording
studio equipment.<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Here’s a few ways you can tell if the equipment is worth the
asking price and will happily meet your needs:<o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Check physical condition
     in the ad: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Always
     assess the physical condition in the ad or ask the seller to describe the
     condition. <o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ensure the item is as
     described: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Check
     for scratches, dents, loose items, and quality. <o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Pay attention to brand
     name: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">It’s
     worthwhile to note that brand names the likes of Shure, Neumann, and
     Digi002 are high-end, and may command a higher price. <o:p></o:p></span></li>
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Get an idea of how the
     equipment has been used: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Ask where the item was purchased, how much it was
     purchased for, and when it was purchased.<o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Knowing these variables will give you the leverage you need
to decide whether the the equipment is priced fairly. You can also request a
copy of the original receipt. Ask for original boxes, warranties, and user
guides. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><u>Bill DeWees<o:p></o:p></u></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=_hBVkpHaDQU&amp;feature=emb_rel_end">Home
Voice Over Studio . . . on the cheap! </a><o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Foam Mattress Toppers<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Moving Blankets<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Microphone<o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="circle">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Large Diaphragm Studio Mic / USB Mics work for
      Auditions but not final product. <o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="square">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level3 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.5in;">Marshall MXL 2001 $60.00<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Small Mixer with PreAmp<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Digital Audio Interface (DAI) <o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="circle">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Lexicon Alpha<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Computer Fan Noise can interfere, remove from
     recording space.<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Audacity (Free)<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Headphones (cheap is fine, don’t worry about it). <o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Record Audio Books, it’s the fastest way to get
     going.<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=-eRLVGoCAq8">DON'T
Get Into the Voice Over Business! (...until you've watched this video) </a><o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">It’s WORK, not just play. <o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="font-size: 16.0pt;"><a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=j3DczOxOh7Q">How to Build a Voice Over
Business - Webinar Recording </a>&nbsp;<o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Notes: 30:31<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><u><span style="font-size: 20.0pt;">Tech<o:p></o:p></span></u></b></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Bad Audio kills business. Expensive Microphone does
     not equal great audio. <o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">A great space creates great audio. Treat your Audio
     Space.<o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="circle">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;"><b>Sound Proofing</b> (Outside Noise/Laundry
      Machine/Traffic/Kids Playing, noise from outside of the room). <o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;"><b>Sound Treatment</b> (Room Noise/Echo)<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Microphone: <o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="circle">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;"><b>Fifine K670</b><o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Aliso Creek Voice Over Classes: <a href="https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=v5pLly8m-nA">USB Microphones for
     Voice Over Recording</a> <o:p></o:p></li>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="circle">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">All Sound is Analog.<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">All Audio is Digital.<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Interface Converts Analog to Digital and Back.<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">USB Mics in the old days didn’t record well. New
      ones are sufficient for most recording. <o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Large Diaphragm <o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">20Hz-20Khz<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Gain Control<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Zero Latency Monitoring (Headphone Jack on Mic)<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">24 Bit Mic Chipset<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level2 lfo2; tab-stops: list 1.0in;">Aliso Creek Voice Over Classes: FREE ebook: Your
      Voice Over GPS: <a href="http://alisocreek.net/voiceover.html">http://alisocreek.net/voiceover.html</a>
      Should you use a USB mic for voice over recording? With the latest in
      technology available, the answer may surprise. you!<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><a href="https://www.bluedesigns.com/products/yeti/">BLUE
     Yeti USB Specs</a><o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="text-indent: .5in;">
<!--[if gte vml 1]><v:shapetype
 id="_x0000_t75" coordsize="21600,21600" o:spt="75" o:preferrelative="t"
 path="m@4@5l@4@11@9@11@9@5xe" filled="f" stroked="f">
 <v:stroke joinstyle="miter"/>
 <v:formulas>
  <v:f eqn="if lineDrawn pixelLineWidth 0"/>
  <v:f eqn="sum @0 1 0"/>
  <v:f eqn="sum 0 0 @1"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @2 1 2"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @3 21600 pixelWidth"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @3 21600 pixelHeight"/>
  <v:f eqn="sum @0 0 1"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @6 1 2"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @7 21600 pixelWidth"/>
  <v:f eqn="sum @8 21600 0"/>
  <v:f eqn="prod @7 21600 pixelHeight"/>
  <v:f eqn="sum @10 21600 0"/>
 </v:formulas>
 <v:path o:extrusionok="f" gradientshapeok="t" o:connecttype="rect"/>
 <o:lock v:ext="edit" aspectratio="t"/>
</v:shapetype><v:shape id="Picture_x0020_1" o:spid="_x0000_i1025" type="#_x0000_t75"
 alt="" style='width:497.25pt;height:285.75pt'>
 <v:imagedata src="file:///C:/Users/darre/AppData/Local/Temp/msohtmlclip1/01/clip_image001.jpg"
  o:href="cid:image001.jpg@01D5FC6B.4C5903E0"/>
</v:shape><![endif]--><!--[if !vml]--><img border="0" height="381" src="file:///C:/Users/darre/AppData/Local/Temp/msohtmlclip1/01/clip_image001.jpg" style="height: 3.968in; width: 6.895in;" v:shapes="Picture_x0020_1" width="663" /><!--[endif]--><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Ns1 Plugin by Waves.com<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;"><a href="https://www.waves.com/plugins/ns1-noise-suppressor#How-to-Clean-Up-Noisy-Audio-in-Videos">NS1
     Noise Suppressor</a><o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Reg. $149 66% OFF On Sale $49.99<o:p></o:p></li>
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">The most intuitive professional noise suppression
     plugin ever created, Waves NS1 Noise Suppressor intelligently
     differentiates between dialog and unwanted noise. Perfect for post
     production, audio forensics and musical applications alike, NS1 instantly
     analyzes and adapts to your signal in real-time, bringing the foreground
     into focus as it eliminates unnecessary background noise. Controlled by a
     simple single fader, NS1 also features an attenuation meter which shows
     you just how much overall energy is being removed from your input signal.
     With NS1, noise suppression is as easy as fading away.<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal" style="text-indent: .5in;">
<!--[if gte vml 1]><v:shape id="Picture_x0020_2"
 o:spid="_x0000_i1026" type="#_x0000_t75" alt="" style='width:381pt;height:212.25pt'>
 <v:imagedata src="file:///C:/Users/darre/AppData/Local/Temp/msohtmlclip1/01/clip_image002.jpg"
  o:href="cid:image002.jpg@01D5FC6B.4C5903E0"/>
</v:shape><![endif]--><!--[if !vml]--><img border="0" height="283" src="file:///C:/Users/darre/AppData/Local/Temp/msohtmlclip1/01/clip_image002.jpg" style="height: 2.947in; width: 5.291in;" v:shapes="Picture_x0020_2" width="508" /><!--[endif]--><o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><u><span style="font-size: 20.0pt;">Performance<o:p></o:p></span></u></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">Real People, Guy/Girl Next Door. Natural Voice. No
     Announcers. Upbeat. Not Sales. Genuine. Authentic. <o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><u><span style="font-size: 20.0pt;">Business (Marketing)<o:p></o:p></span></u></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoListParagraph" style="margin-left: 0in; mso-list: l4 level1 lfo2; tab-stops: list .5in;">You are responsible for Marketing yourself. Don’t
     rely on an agent or agency. Market yourself. Create your own brand. Build
     a website, get a Demo Reel, etc.<o:p></o:p></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<b><span style="font-size: 20.0pt;"><a href="https://www.voices.com/help/beginners-guide-to-voice-acting">Beginner's
Guide to Voice Acting</a><o:p></o:p></span></b></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
1. Get to Know Your Voice Through Voice Acting Practice<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
2. Learn and Try the 5 Basic Types of Characters in Voice
Acting Roles<o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
3. Regularly Get Voice Acting Practice with Sample Scripts <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Let's explore the roles in detail. <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="1" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l3 level1 lfo3; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The Instructor </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
When teaching someone, for example, in a corporate training
video or children's game, the voice over best suited for this kind of project
is a straightforward, educated, and instructional voice. The role of this
particular voice talent is to teach or provide information to fulfill a
specific goal or purpose. Anyone who has had the opportunity to run a class,
guide presentations, or has managed a team will likely find this role
comfortable. <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="2" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l3 level1 lfo3; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="background: yellow; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-highlight: yellow;">The Real Person </span></b><span style="background: yellow; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-highlight: yellow;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="background: yellow; mso-highlight: yellow;">Projects
requiring a more casual approach often benefit from relatable, genuine voice
overs. These are referred to as ‘real person’ voice overs, commonly known as
the ‘regular guy’ or the ‘girl next door.’ This character is homegrown,
sensible, and friendly, with a touch of familiarity, providing a more intimate
interpretation of the script that instills trust. <o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="background: yellow; mso-highlight: yellow;">While
this role may seem like it should suit everyone, it is harder than one might
think to read a script and still sound like you're having a regular
conversation with a friend or loved one. <o:p></o:p></span></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="background: yellow; mso-highlight: yellow;">Former
radio personalities tend to need help in this area because they've worked for
so long in an industry that’s challenged them to change the timbre of their
voices to sound like an announcer (see more about the announcer below).</span> <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="3" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l3 level1 lfo3; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The Spokesperson </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
A spokesperson can be on-camera or off-camera, depending on
the medium you are using. The role of a spokesperson is generally played by a
confident, charismatic person able to promote a cause, product, or service with
ease and authority. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
A voice over of this nature needs to be driven, optimistic
and assured. If you've ever been in sales, these traits probably sound
familiar. Many people who gravitate to voice overs from sales positions tend to
feel right at home in this role. <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="4" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l3 level1 lfo3; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="background: yellow; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-highlight: yellow;">The Narrator </span></b><span style="background: yellow; mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;; mso-highlight: yellow;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<span style="background: yellow; mso-highlight: yellow;">Storytelling
is where the Narrator is most at home. Omniscient, unbiased, courteous and
honest, a narrator's job is to provide an audio landscape for a listener,
briefing them on background information, posing questions, and providing
solutions as they guide their audience through a program or documentary.
Narrators can be male or female, and the most important factors are that they
can communicate clearly and engagingly. If people hang on your every word while
listening to you tell a story then this is likely your comfort zone.</span> <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="5" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l3 level1 lfo3; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">The Announcer </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
The announcer, often heard live at events, on commercials,
promos or introducing segments for radio or podcasts, is a product of the
broadcast age, most celebrated at its height in the Golden Age of Radio and
early television broadcasts. Announcers can introduce an idea and assertively
make a call for action at the conclusion of a commercial advertisement or short
video. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
If you were in radio broadcasting and you're now pursuing a
career in voice over, then you may find this voice over character fits you like
a glove. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo4; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Character information:</span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"> <o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Who is the character? What’s the character’s age, background
and&nbsp;history? <o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo4; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Plot information: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
What happened or will happen to the character? Who else is
with them in the scene? Where is the action taking&nbsp;place? <o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo4; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Voice over style and
     tone:</span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"> <o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
How should the character’s voice sound? Is the voice over
role for a child or adult? What are the character’s&nbsp;emotions? <o:p></o:p></div>
<ul style="margin-top: 0in;" type="disc">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l0 level1 lfo4; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Artistic directions: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ul>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Look for artistic directions that let you know more about
the setting, your character’s position, etc. Are certain words meant to be
emphasized? You must carefully read the lines to look for italicized or bold
words and understand what they&nbsp;mean. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
The Top 3 Tips for Reading a Voice Acting&nbsp;Script <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="1" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l2 level1 lfo5; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Stay relaxed: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
It is crucial to stay relaxed while reading and performing
with a script. This doesn’t necessarily mean that you should read more slowly,
but rather with a calm and focused mind. This will help you reduce loud
breathing sounds and silly&nbsp;mistakes. <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="2" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l2 level1 lfo5; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Read the script first:</span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"> <o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
Needless to say, if you get the script before hand, you
should always go through it a few times before the recording session. This way,
you’ll be familiar with the lines and not miss important stylistic notes
and&nbsp;pauses. <o:p></o:p></div>
<ol start="3" style="margin-top: 0in;" type="1">
<li class="MsoNormal" style="mso-list: l2 level1 lfo5; tab-stops: list .5in;"><b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;">Move freely: </span></b><span style="mso-fareast-font-family: &quot;Times New Roman&quot;;"><o:p></o:p></span></li>
</ol>
<div class="MsoNormal">
If your recording space allows you to stand and move around
in the booth, that’s excellent. Standing helps you breathe more clearly. It
also helps you get into character and make natural movements while
voice&nbsp;acting. <o:p></o:p></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
<br /></div>
<div class="MsoNormal">
........ TBC</div>
